title: idesync VSCode config
description: Pass-through settings for VSCode-family editors (VS Code, Insiders, VSCodium, Cursor, Windsurf), applied by idesync (the `vsc` editor). Nothing is translated: these are raw VSCode values applied to every targeted editor.

targets.description: Restrict to these editors by name. Empty = every discovered VSCode-family editor.

settings.description: Top-level settings.json keys to set. Every other key, plus comments and formatting, is preserved — only these keys are (re)written.

keybindings.description: The keybindings.json array. When present, idesync OWNS keybindings.json (like a generated keymap — seed it with `create`); absent = left untouched. A `mod` modifier token is resolved on apply to the target host's primary modifier (Ctrl on Linux/Windows, Cmd on macOS), written directly into `key` — VSCode's user keybindings.json has no per-entry platform override field, so the same config produces a different `key` on each host it's applied to.

key.description: Keystroke, e.g. "ctrl+shift+p". Use `mod` for the platform-primary modifier (Ctrl on Linux/Windows, Cmd on macOS); `ctrl` stays literal everywhere.

install.description: Marketplace extension IDs (publisher.name) to ensure installed via the editor CLI. Only missing ones are installed.

local.description: Locally-installed (non-marketplace) extensions bundled as .vsix files by `create` (on by default; disable with --no-local). Each is installed from its bundled file via the editor CLI when the id is missing; skip on apply/check with --no-local.

id.description: Extension ID (publisher.name), used for the already-installed check.

vsix.description: Path to the bundled .vsix, relative to this config file.
